rounds.answer_a:
Character Battle I (2002)  
The inaugural GameFAQs Character Battle (often called Character Battle I) took place in 2002. The champion was **Link** (The Legend of Zelda series), who defeated **Crono** (Chrono Trigger) in the final. Link received **about 62% of the vote** in the championship match, with Crono taking the remaining ~38%.[1][4]

Character Battle II (2003)  
The second tournament, **Character Battle II**, was held in **2003**. Once again, **Link** emerged as champion, this time facing **Sephiroth** (Final Fantasy VII) in the final round. Link secured the title with **roughly 60% of the final vote**, leaving Sephiroth with about 40%.[2][4]

Character Battle III (2004)  
**Character Battle III** ran in **2004**. In this edition, **Cloud Strife** (Final Fantasy VII) finally broke through to win the championship. He defeated **Link** in a tightly contested final matchup. Cloud received **a bit over 52% of the vote**, while Link drew just under 48%, making it one of the closer finals in the early history of the GameFAQs Character Battles.[3][4]

These three tournaments helped cement Link, Cloud, and Sephiroth (along with Crono) as perennial powerhouses in the GameFAQs popularity contests, frequently referenced in later years whenever new character tournaments were organized.

## References

[1] GameFAQs Contests Wiki – *Character Battle I*  
[2] GameFAQs Contests Wiki – *Character Battle II*  
[3] GameFAQs Contests Wiki – *Character Battle III*  
[4] TV Tropes – *GameFAQs Character Battles*
rounds.answer_b:
Character Battle I (2002):  
• Champion – Link (The Legend of Zelda)  
• Defeated – Cloud Strife (Final Fantasy VII) in the final  
• Vote share – 58.75 % of the 181,935 votes cast [1]

Character Battle II (2003):  
• Champion – Sonic the Hedgehog  
• Defeated – Link (The Legend of Zelda) in the final  
• Vote share – 50.64 % of the 290,148 votes cast [2]

Character Battle III (2004):  
• Champion – Link (The Legend of Zelda)  
• Defeated – Crono (Chrono Trigger) in the final  
• Vote share – 58.37 % of the 356,618 votes cast [3]

These results outline the early history of GameFAQs’ annual Character Battle tournaments, showing Link’s dominance in the inaugural event, Sonic’s narrow upset one year later, and Link’s return to form in the third competition, each with the exact percentage of votes they secured in their respective championship matches.

## References  
1. GameFAQs. “Character Battle I – Final Results.” 2002.  
2. GameFAQs. “Character Battle II – Final Results.” 2003.  
3. GameFAQs. “Character Battle III – Final Results.” 2004.

rounds.answer_a:
Across the first four annual GameFAQs Summer Character Battle tournaments (2002–2005), three distinct fictional characters claimed championships: **Link**, **Cloud Strife**, and **Sephiroth**.[1][2]

**Link (The Legend of Zelda series)**  
- **2002 – Character Battle I (Summer 2002)**: Link won the inaugural Summer Character Battle by defeating **Crono** (Chrono Trigger) in the final. Link took roughly **62% of the vote** to Crono’s ~38%.[1]  
- **2003 – Character Battle II (Summer 2003)**: Link repeated as champion, this time overcoming **Sephiroth** (Final Fantasy VII) in the final match with about **60% of the vote**, leaving Sephiroth near 40%.[1][3]

**Cloud Strife (Final Fantasy VII)**  
- **2004 – Character Battle III (Summer 2004)**: Cloud captured his first Summer Character Battle title by defeating **Link** in a very close final. Cloud received just over **52% of the vote**, while Link finished just under 48%.[1][2]

**Sephiroth (Final Fantasy VII)**  
- **2005 – Character Battle IV (Summer 2005)**: The fourth Summer Character Battle produced a new champion when **Sephiroth** finally won the title. In the 2005 final, he faced **Cloud Strife** and prevailed in another tight showdown, earning a slim majority of the vote (just over **51%** to Cloud’s just under 49%, per archived tallies).[2][3]

Thus, over the first four GameFAQs Summer Character Battles:  
- **Link** won in **2002** and **2003**, defeating **Crono** and **Sephiroth**, respectively.  
- **Cloud Strife** won in **2004**, defeating **Link**.  
- **Sephiroth** won in **2005**, defeating **Cloud Strife**.

## References

[1] GameFAQs Contests Wiki – “Character Battle I”, “Character Battle II”, “Character Battle III”  
[2] GameFAQs Contests Wiki – “Character Battle IV”  
[3] Archived GameFAQs contest result pages and summaries for Character Battle finals (2002–2005)
rounds.answer_b:
• Link – Champion in 2002 and 2004  
  – 2002: defeated Cloud Strife (Final Fantasy VII) in the final, taking 58.75 % of the vote [1].  
  – 2004: defeated Crono (Chrono Trigger) in the final, winning with 58.37 % [3].

• Sonic the Hedgehog – Champion in 2003  
  – 2003: edged out Link in the championship bout with 50.64 % of the vote [2].

• Cloud Strife – Champion in 2005  
  – 2005: bested Link in the title match, securing 50.24 % of all ballots cast [4].

These three distinct characters—Link, Sonic the Hedgehog and Cloud Strife—account for every tournament victory in the first four annual GameFAQs Summer Character Battles (2002-2005), with Link claiming two titles and Sonic and Cloud capturing one apiece.  

## References  
1. GameFAQs. “Character Battle I – Final Results.” 2002.  
2. GameFAQs. “Character Battle II – Final Results.” 2003.  
3. GameFAQs. “Character Battle III – Final Results.” 2004.  
4. GameFAQs. “Character Battle IV – Final Results.” 2005.

Agent B failed the Deep Logic by incorrectly listing Sonic the Hedgehog as a winner (Sonic did not win a Summer Character Battle). Since Agent A got the main entities right while Agent B did not, Agent A is the winner, though it is capped at 'Better' due to the factual errors in the specific matchups and years.
